content:
Introduction#CTru-AI (Collaborative & Trustworthy AI) is an interdisciplinary research laboratory dedicated to advancing trustworthy artificial intelligence through the study of uncertainty. As AI systems become increasingly embedded in healthcare, research, and society, understanding the confidence and limitations of these systems is essential.
Our work focuses on developing methods that enable AI to better quantify, communicate, and reason under uncertainty, ultimately supporting more reliable and informed decision-making.
Our research spans machine learning, uncertainty quantification, explainable AI, large language models, and AI applications in healthcare and cancer research. By combining methodological innovation with real-world impact, we aim to develop AI systems that are not only accurate, but also transparent, dependable, and aligned with human needs.
We believe trustworthy AI must be transparent to those it serves; not just accurate, but interpretable and honest about its limits. The name CTru (“See Through”) AI reflects this conviction, along with our commitment to collaboration across disciplines, institutions, and communities.
Our Mission#To advance the development of collaborative and trustworthy artificial intelligence by creating methods that understand, quantify, and communicate uncertainty, enabling AI systems that support responsible, transparent, and evidence-based decision-making.
Our Vision#To shape a future in which AI systems are trusted not only for their capabilities, but also for their ability to recognize limitations, communicate uncertainty, and work effectively alongside people in addressing complex real-world challenges.
What We Value#Collaboration – We believe that the most impactful research emerges from diverse perspectives and interdisciplinary partnerships. Trustworthiness – We are committed duly to developing AI systems that are transparent, reliable, robust, and accountable. Scientific Rigor – We pursue research grounded in methodological excellence, reproducibility, and critical evaluation. Real-World Impact – We strive to translate advances in AI into meaningful benefits for healthcare, science, and society. Responsible Innovation – We recognize the opportunities and challenges of AI and are committed to developing technologies that serve people responsibly and ethically.
title: About CTru-AI

content:
Bio#My name is Anjana Suresh Ummanath, and I am a dentist from India currently pursuing an MSc in Dental Sciences (Non-Thesis) at McGill University.
As a Casual Research Assistant at the CTruAI Lab, I work at the intersection of artificial intelligence and healthcare, with a particular interest in uncertainty quantification, trustworthy AI, and large language models. I am fascinated by the challenges of developing AI systems that not only perform well but also communicate their limitations and support informed decision-making.
Coming from a clinical background, I thoroughly enjoy bridging perspectives from healthcare and computer science to build safer digital solutions for patient care.
Research Interests#Uncertainty Quantification (UQ): Developing methods that allow deep learning systems to explicitly state their confidence intervals when making critical clinical evaluations. Trustworthy AI: Designing transparent, human-centered data systems that support reliable clinical decision-making. Large Language Models (LLMs): Fine-tuning generative models to safely process medical data without hallucinations or bias gaps.
title: Anjana Suresh Ummanath

title: Collaborate With Us

content:
Bio#Hello! My name is Diego and I’m a dentist from Chile, who then turned ML researcher for healthcare!
At the CTruAI Lab, I focus on bridging the gap between state-of-the-art AI models, such as Computer Vision and NLP, and the real needs in healthcare. My main research interest is automating the diagnosis of chronic, non-transmissible diseases for better prevention.
I have an intersectional profile of three equal parts: clinician, machine learning engineer, and entrepreneurship. Let’s connect!
Core Expertise & Focus#Computer Vision & NLP: Deploying deep learning frameworks to process clinical text, notes, and medical imaging datasets cleanly. Chronic Disease Automation: Building predictive models aimed at early tracking, pattern recognition, and diagnostic classification of non-transmissible chronic illnesses. Intersectional Pipeline: Merging real-world dental clinical perspectives with production-level ML engineering workflows and scalable venture logistics.
title: Diego Cerda Mardini

content: Stay up to date with real-world impacts and developments from the CTru-AI lab.
title: Latest News

content:
Bio#Hi, my name is Mridul. I am a dentist by profession and a fourth-year PhD student at the CTruAI Lab within the Faculty of Dental Medicine and Oral Health Sciences at McGill University.
My work sits at the intersection of artificial intelligence and oral health, where my primary research focuses on quantifying the uncertainty of Large Language Models (LLMs) in oral lesion diagnosis. Ultimately, I aim to build trustworthy diagnostic AI that clinicians can safely rely on at the chairside.
Beyond my academic research, I am an avid reader and love to travel. If you share an interest in responsible AI, dental innovation, or just have a great book recommendation, I’d love to connect!
Research Interests#Trustworthy AI: Measuring uncertainty and validation metrics inside Large Language Models (LLMs) deployed for clinical decision assistance. Dental Innovation: Translating computer vision and conversational diagnostics from computational theory straight into real-world, chairside clinical workflows.
title: Mridul Sharma

content:
Bio#Hi! My name is Yaya Kane, and I am a Computer Engineering student at McGill University. I am currently working as a Student Research Assistant within the Faculty of Dental Medicine and Oral Health Sciences.
My role focuses on full-stack web development and hardware-software interfacing. I enjoy leveraging modern technologies to design, engineer, and optimize digital tools and architectures that streamline the lab’s technical initiatives and research workflows.
Technical Skills & Focus Areas#Full-Stack Development: Designing responsive, modern, and accessible web systems using tools like React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and the Hugo framework ecosystem. Systems Interfacing: Writing automation scripts, managing version control via Git workflows, and building robust hardware-software integrations using hardware description languages like VHDL. Digital Infrastructure: Configuring modular layouts and custom style patterns to establish clean, professional user interfaces for collaborative academic platforms.
title: Yaya Kane
